items.question_text: Complete the prescribed traffic-information phraseology: "TRAFFIC TWELVE OCLOCK SEVEN MILES EASTBOUND GULFSTREAM 650 ONE _____ THOUSAND"
items.explanation: Per 5-1-4, the prescribed phraseology is "...ONE SEVEN THOUSAND."

choices.text: SEVEN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed emergency code phraseology: "SQUAWK _____ ON 7700"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-3, the prescribed phraseology is "SQUAWK MAYDAY ON 7700."

choices.text: MAYDAY

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed transponder-reset phraseology: "IDENTIFICATION RESET TRANSPONDER _____ APPROPRIATE CODE"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-12, the prescribed phraseology uses "SQUAWK."

choices.text: SQUAWK

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed Mode C validation phraseology: "VERIFY USING TWO NINER NINER TWO AS YOUR _____ SETTING"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-15, the prescribed phraseology uses "ALTIMETER."

choices.text: ALTIMETER

items.question_text: Which event requires you to validate a Mode C altitude readout?
items.explanation: Per 5-2-15, validation is required on an initial track start, a start from coast or frozen status, or after an unreliable Mode C readout.

choices.text: An initial track start, a start from coast or frozen status, or an unreliable Mode C readout.
Every time the pilot checks on frequency, with no exceptions.
Only after the aircraft reports leaving cruise altitude.
Only when the aircraft enters Class A airspace.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altitude verification phraseology: "_____ AT ALTITUDE/FLIGHT LEVEL"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-16, the prescribed phraseology begins with "VERIFY."

choices.text: VERIFY

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altitude-maintenance phraseology: "NEGATIVE CLIMB/DESCEND AND _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-17, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed ADS-B outage phraseology: "STOP ADS-B TRANSMISSIONS AND IF ABLE _____ THREE/ALFA CODE"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-22, the prescribed phraseology uses "SQUAWK."

choices.text: SQUAWK

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed ADS-B outage phraseology: "STOP ADS-B TRANSMISSIONS AND IF ABLE _____ THREE/ALFA CODE"
items.explanation: Per 5-2-23, the prescribed phraseology uses "SQUAWK."

choices.text: SQUAWK

items.question_text: Which is an approved method for transferring radar identification?
items.explanation: Per 5-4-3, approved methods include landline voice communications, physical pointing, and approved automation capabilities.

choices.text: Use landline voice communications, physical pointing, or approved automation capabilities.
Rely on pilot position reports alone without controller-to-controller transfer.
Use tower Quick Look functions whenever convenient, even without a facility directive.
Transfer identity only after the aircraft changes to the new frequency.

items.question_text: Once a handoff has been initiated or accepted, when may the transferring controller change the aircraft's flight path, altitude, speed, or data block without further approval?
items.explanation: Per 5-4-5, the receiving controller's approval is required unless otherwise coordinated or specified in an LOA or facility directive.

choices.text: Only when otherwise coordinated or specified in an LOA or facility directive; otherwise the receiving controller's approval must be obtained first.
Any time before the pilot checks on with the receiving controller.
Any time the aircraft remains above the MVA.
Only after the aircraft physically enters the receiving sector.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed traffic-report phraseology: "_____ PASSING THE TRAFFIC"
items.explanation: Per 5-5-7, the prescribed phraseology begins with "REPORT."

choices.text: REPORT

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed vector phraseology: "_____ LEFT/RIGHT HEADING DEGREES"
items.explanation: Per 5-6-2, the prescribed phraseology begins with "TURN."

choices.text: TURN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altitude-assignment phraseology: "DESCEND AND _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 5-7-2, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed departure-separation vector phraseology: "_____ LEFT/RIGHT HEADING DEGREES"
items.explanation: Per 5-8-2, the prescribed phraseology begins with "TURN."

choices.text: TURN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed arrival-advisory phraseology: "_____ VECTORS ACROSS FINAL FOR PURPOSE"
items.explanation: Per 5-9-3, the prescribed phraseology begins with "EXPECT."

choices.text: EXPECT

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed safety-alert phraseology: "TRAFFIC ALERT CALL SIGN TURN RIGHT/LEFT IMMEDIATELY HEADING DEGREES CLIMB/DESCEND AND _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 5-9-7, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed safety-alert phraseology: "TRAFFIC ALERT CALL SIGN TURN LEFT/RIGHT IMMEDIATELY HEADING DEGREES CLIMB AND _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 5-9-9, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed safety-alert phraseology: "TRAFFIC ALERT CALL SIGN TURN LEFT/RIGHT IMMEDIATELY HEADING DEGREES CLIMB AND _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 5-9-10, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed airport-information phraseology: "NO TRAFFIC OR LANDING RUNWAY INFORMATION _____ FOR THE AIRPORT"
items.explanation: Per 5-10-2, the prescribed phraseology uses "AVAILABLE."

choices.text: AVAILABLE

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed nonradar-approach phraseology: "PROCEED WITH NONRADAR APPROACH _____ ALTITUDE UNTIL ESTABLISHED ON/OVER FIX/NAVAID/ APPROACH PROCEDURE"
items.explanation: Per 5-10-4, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed surveillance-approach missed-approach briefing: "YOUR MISSED APPROACH PROCEDURE IS _____ APPROACH PROCEDURE"
items.explanation: Per 5-10-11, the prescribed phraseology uses "MISSED."

choices.text: MISSED

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed surveillance-approach heading phraseology: "TURN RIGHT OR LEFT HEADING DEGREES /FLY _____ HEADING"
items.explanation: Per 5-10-12, the prescribed phraseology uses "RUNWAY."

choices.text: RUNWAY

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed PAR phraseology: "_____ RUNWAY APPROACH/RUNWAY LIGHTS OR AIRPORT IN SIGHT"
items.explanation: Per 5-11-2, the prescribed phraseology begins with "REPORT."

choices.text: REPORT

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed PAR phraseology: "PREPARE TO _____ IN NUMBER MILES"
items.explanation: Per 5-11-3, the prescribed phraseology uses "DESCEND."

choices.text: DESCEND

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed PAR phraseology: "NUMBER MILES FROM RUNWAY/AIRPORT/HELIPORT OR _____ APPROACH POINT"
items.explanation: Per 5-11-5, the prescribed phraseology uses "MISSED."

choices.text: MISSED

items.question_text: If ATC initiates a delay and issues holding instructions with an EFC other than "no delay expected," what must be entered into ERAM?
items.explanation: Per 5-13-9, a hold message is required, with EFC and any non-published holding information entered by the approved methods.

choices.text: A hold message, with EFC and any non-published holding information entered by the approved methods.
Only a free-text remark in the fourth line of the data block.
Only the aircraft type and route of flight, because the hold is implied by delay status.
Nothing, because ERAM can infer the hold from the track history.

items.question_text: If "SAA DOWN" is displayed in the Outage View, what must sector controllers do?
items.explanation: Per 5-13-10, controllers must manually create visual indicators on the display to reflect changes in special activity airspace status.

choices.text: Manually create visual indicators on the situation display to reflect changes to special activity airspace status.
Assume the displayed SAA status will continue updating automatically.
Ignore special activity airspace status until the outage clears.
Terminate all IFR operations that could be affected by special activity airspace.

items.question_text: What may STARS be used for under this application paragraph?
items.explanation: Per 5-14-1, STARS may be used to identify aircraft assigned a discrete beacon code, maintain target identity, and perform handoffs between controllers.

choices.text: Identifying aircraft assigned a discrete beacon code, maintaining target identity, and performing handoffs of those targets between controllers.
Authorizing pilots to deviate from IFR altitude requirements.
Replacing all local directives for terminal operations.
Automatically issuing takeoff and landing clearances.

items.question_text: If it becomes necessary to suspend tracks, which tracks must be suspended first?
items.explanation: Per 5-14-8, tracks not displaying automatic altitude readouts must be suspended first if suspension becomes necessary.

choices.text: Those not displaying automatic altitude readouts.
Those displaying automatic altitude readouts first, because they take more display space.
Only VFR tracks outside Class B airspace.
Only tracks that have already been handed off.

items.question_text: If the track-suspend condition still exists after the first suspensions, which tracks may then be suspended?
items.explanation: Per 5-14-8, if the condition still exists, tracks displaying automatic altitude readouts may then be suspended.

choices.text: Those displaying automatic altitude readouts.
No additional tracks; only the first group may ever be suspended.
Only tracks already in coast status.
Only tracks below the MVA.
